response:
The fare depends on the total distance driven and any waiting time converted into extra kilometers. If the distance is four kilometers or less then the cost remains fixed at ten yuan. For distances beyond four kilometers the price increases by two yuan for each additional kilometer. This calculation includes any waiting periods that the city rules convert into equivalent driving distance. The spring season often brings more tourists to the airport which increases the number of trips.

We must calculate the maximum waiting time when the actual distance was fifteen kilometers and the fare was thirty-eight yuan. The base fare covers the first four kilometers so the remaining twenty-eight yuan comes from the extra distance. Dividing twenty-eight by the two yuan rate shows that twelve extra kilometers were charged beyond the initial four. The total charged distance is therefore sixteen kilometers while the actual physical distance was only fifteen kilometers. This one kilometer difference represents the waiting time converted by the city rules. Since five minutes of waiting equals one kilometer the taxi stopped for at most five minutes. The spring weather might cause traffic delays that add to this waiting time.
